title: NFP
intro: The March payrolls print significantly exceeded expectations by 41K jobs while marking a notable acceleration from February's upwardly revised 151K, signaling robust labor market momentum that undermines recent Fed dovish pivot expectations. USD is rallying across the board with DXY pushing through
bodyHtml:
The March payrolls print significantly exceeded expectations by 41K jobs while marking a notable acceleration from February's upwardly revised 151K, signaling robust labor market momentum that undermines recent Fed dovish pivot expectations. USD is rallying across the board with DXY pushing through 104.50 resistance as 2-year yields spike 8bps on repricing of June rate cut probabilities from 75% to sub-50%.

Equity futures are under pressure with NDX leading losses as growth stocks reprice higher terminal rates, while financials outperform on steepening yield curve dynamics.
The print reinforces our view that the Fed will maintain restrictive policy longer than consensus expects, with Chair Powell's Jackson Hole dovish tone now looking premature given persistent labor market tightness.

 Key tail risk remains a follow-through acceleration in wage growth data due Friday, which could force complete repricing of the easing cycle and trigger broader risk-off across duration and growth assets.
